transcript: This is Story Recapped. Today, I'm going to explain a horror and thriller film called I Spit on Your Grave 2. It is a standalone sequel to the previous film of the same name, showing a series of vengeful acts of a woman robbed of her dignity. Spoilers ahead. Watch out, and take care. In a world where crimes are at large, no one is really safe. Even the corners of a home are not enough to guarantee one's safety. For the film's main character, Katie, takes a risk to make ends meet. This is a struggle that she will face in the most unlikely and unconventional way. However, once justice comes to her terms, she holds nothing back. Katie Carter is an aspiring model from Missouri and is a part-time worker at a restaurant in New York City. She meets with an agent, Sharon, who informs her that she must update her portfolio to land modeling jobs. It will cost Katie more money, but she takes criticism well. On her way out, Katie finds a free photography poster and takes the phone number. Katie makes ends meet by balancing jobs, and she lives in an apartment complex in the heart of the city. She is acquainted with Jason, her neighbor, and helps him around the apartment. In her apartment, Katie browses through her old portfolio and decides to contact the photo advertisement out of desperation. A foreign man answers the phone and urges Katie to send a picture to the man named Ivan. Not long after, she receives a confirmation with the schedule and location of the photoshoot, which makes Katie happy. Given her financial situation, Katie is in desperation. She has bigger dreams, and more than just working at a restaurant, she aspires to make a name for herself. Becoming a model has been her lifelong dream, but it does not come without sacrifices. She left her small town for a big-shot city to pursue her passion, clueless of what it can offer her. The free photo shoot becomes a silver lining for her, and she cannot be more excited to be a step closer to her dreams. The day of the shoot arrives, and Katie travels to the address sent to her. There, she sees a model in lingerie being photographed in the studio. Her excitement slightly wears off upon seeing the studio's interior in condition. Shortly after, she meets photographer siblings, Ivan, Georgie, and Nikolai. Georgie clearly shows interest and infatuation toward Katie. As he assists his brother during the shoot, Ivan suddenly pauses from taking pictures and tells Katie that her photos are not turning out well because of her outfit. He then implies that she should show more skin to make the photos look fiercer, which makes her uncomfortable. From a corner, Nikolai calls her a prude, but Georgie reprimands him for saying such a thing. Despite Ivan's persuasion that showing skin is normal in their industry, Katie walks out of the building after saying that she won't do anything that she is not comfortable with. After several days, Georgie visits Katie in her apartment to hand her a flash drive with her photos. She hesitantly accepts it, but she does not invite Georgie in because of discomfort. That night, Georgie browses through his copy of Katie's pictures, making his obsession toward her grow even more. As Katie is in bed, she hears camera shutters and is awoken by them. She is stunned to see Georgie across her bed secretly documenting her, so she electrocutes him in self-defense. She runs to the door, but Georgie awakes just in time to stop her. He drags Katie back to the room before hitting her head until she collapses from weakness. To prevent suspicious noise, Georgie gags and ties her, resuming his evil plan on Katie. Georgie's crush on Katie turns out to be a toxic obsession. Because of his willingness to spend time with her, he did the unthinkable. He did not take Katie's rejection lightly, and decided that he had to get what he wants. Georgie let his emotions get the best of him, causing him to turn a blind eye to what is right and wrong. He disregards any form of conscience left in him, as he is solely focused on Katie's attention. From below, Jason hears Katie's muffled screams, so he goes to check on her. He is taken aback by the sight that welcomes him inside. Katie, seemingly being held hostage, as he enters, Georgie emerges from a corner and starts stabbing him to death. Being helpless, Katie stares at Jason while Georgie forces himself on her. After his unforgivable act, Georgie realizes the crimes that he has done. He cries in a corner while calling his brother and informs him that he did something bad. After a couple of minutes, Nikolai and Ivan arrive at Katie's apartment, only to find her in a corner of the room crying. The brothers begin to clean up the shreds of evidence of the crime scene and feed Katie ketamine to render her unconscious. Katie wakes up in a run-down basement, unclothed and handcuffed. She finds Nikolai taking advantage of her unconsciousness as he forces himself on her. It only stops when Georgie enters the basement to reprimand his brother. Both of them begin to argue about Katie, but Ivan enters to stop them. He orders his younger brothers to go upstairs before Lit and Katie spend the night alone. The following day, Ivan pours a bucket of water on Katie to clean herself. When she acts aggressively toward Georgie, who intends to bathe her, the other two approach to force ketamine on her again. With this, Georgie approaches her once again to gently wipe her skin. Katie finds a small covered window which gives her hope about her situation. To stay awake, Katie forces the substance out of her system by making herself gag until she spits it out. The last time Katie was unconscious, she suffered things she did not even know about. It created fear within her and insecurity toward the people around her. At this point, she could not let herself become unconscious, thinking that she might not get to wake up again. She becomes desperate of keeping her guards up, even in the slightest way possible. The scene shows how she has not given up hope just yet, and how she is willing to fight back. After some time, Georgie comes back to the basement to clothe Katie in a red dress. Georgie unlocks the handcuffs to properly fit the clothes, and it gives Katie the chance to escape. When Georgie turns his back, Katie hits him with the pail and recklessly hits his head. As he lies on the floor, Katie climbs out of the window to freedom. To her surprise, she is now in an unfamiliar city, and no one is willing to help her. She sees signs in a different language and alphabetic characters, which makes her more shocked and confused. Katie approaches a police officer to take refuge, so Detective Kirill safely takes her in his custody. Inside his car, he informs Katie that she is in Bulgaria before bringing her to the police station. At the moment, Katie has no idea how she got there, and her last memory was being in New York. While being questioned, a woman named Anna Patov, who works for a women's organization center, enters the room. She comforts Katie by telling her that she runs a shelter for women who experience the same things as her. However, Katie calmly asks Anna to bring her to the American embassy immediately. According to Anna, Katie should freshen up before going to the embassy, and they could stop at her house nearby. Katie agrees to her idea, so Anna drives to her house. The friendly interior of the house gives Katie a sense of relief and comfort after everything that she has been through. Anna kindly leads Katie downstairs as she has spare clothes, but Katie faces her biggest fear once more. Before even realizing where she is, the familiar feeling of danger reaches her. Katie's trust toward other people put her in an unthinkable situation once more. She saw the light at the end of the tunnel, but she failed to reach the end. Her glimpse of freedom saddens her upon returning to a place she despised the most. Katie's vulnerability was once again stepped on by people who saw her as an object, instead of a person with dreams and ambitions. Just as she thought that she was safe from the people who wished to harm her, she's back in her idea of hell, the basement. Anna is actually the boy's mother, and her kindness was just an act. She even slaps Katie before Ivan drags her back to the mattress. That night, Katie meets Valko, a close friend of the family. The man starts to intimidate and threaten Katie, as if calling her degrading names is not enough. After he repeatedly electroshocks her, including her private parts, and forces himself on the poor woman. Upstairs, Anna blasts music to stop herself from hearing Katie's screams. She quietly weeps in her room, having realized what she is tolerating, but she remains docile. Once Falco leaves, Katie lies motionless in the basement. Ivan comes back to check on her, and Katie gathers all her strength to softly slap him in the face. It angers him to think that she still has no fear for them, so he brutally beats her, leaving her bruised and bloody. Not long after, she notices the brothers digging a hole in the basement. The trio plans on burying her alive, but Katie has lost all her strength to even react to it. Nikolai rips off her clothes and throws it to Georgie before placing Katie inside the box. Before covering it, Georgie places Katie's crucifix necklace inside as well, and they bury her. Despite Georgie being the cause of the chaos, he still shows remorse for his actions. He was the one who cleaned and clothed Katie when she was in her most vulnerable state, and when she was abused by his brothers. Throughout her stay in the basement, Georgie had been gentle to her unlike the rest of his family. He still feels a sense of guilt for what he has done and caused, but it is too late to turn back. He drops her necklace in the box, displaying his minuscule sympathy for her. Inside, all that Katie can do is cry. She cannot scream or move because of her injuries that were incurred from the ordeal. She only listens to the soil being used to cover the box above, and after some time, the brothers finish the job. Georgie comes upstairs to be with his mother who comforts him about the situation. On the other hand, Ivan and Nikolai are outside talking about their plans on hiding and settling down. Just as Katie thought that the box would be her final resting place, the ground below collapses, freeing her from the box. She falls into the sewer system below, where she takes time to recover. Unclothed and hungry, Katie uses all the resources available underground to provide food and shelter during her stay. The lady then discussed the matter with her husband, who was very happy to hear the news. The lady then discussed the matter with her husband, who was very happy to hear the news. The lady then discussed the matter with her husband, who was very happy to hear the news. The lady then discussed the matter with her husband, who was very happy to hear the news. The lady then discussed the matter with her husband, who was takes time to recover. Unclothed and hungry, Katie uses all the resources available underground to provide food and shelter during her stay. Katie then discovers that the sewer leads to a nearby church, where she steals food. She meets Father Dimov, the rector, who catches her stealing food in his pantry. The priest offers her food on his table, but Katie refuses to stay and spend time with him. Because of her decision, he lets her take food, clothes, and a Bible instead. At daybreak, Katie finally comes outside to make her way to the American Embassy. As she nears the building, she decides to turn back and stay. To Father Dimov's surprise, Katie is inside the church, so he approaches her. The priest recognizes her as a crime survivor, so he offers help and support. He suggests that they should contact authorities, but Katie stops him from doing so. When Katie exits, Father Dimov notices that she left a page of the Bible open with the phrase, Vengeance is mine. As a priest, Father Dimov highly values forgiveness. However, he does not force Katie to forgive anyone whenever they interact. He only reminds her of Christian values despite her obvious refusals. Father Dimov also does not question her about her struggles despite knowing what she has been through. The opening verse in the film also shows how Katie has faith but her hunger for revenge is greater. The following day Katie starts her plan for revenge. Her first step is to steal money from Anna's house to buy supplies and weapons. She does this successfully and moves on to her next ploy. Katie waits for Georgie to settle at a restaurant. She quickly walks past his table and puts down his drink. Moments later, Georgie sips his drink and notices that an object was inside the cup. To his surprise, Georgie sees Katie's crucifix pendant in his drink and realizes that she is alive. From a corner, Katie subtly reveals herself and Georgie follows her to make sure that she is actually real. Katie successfully lures Georgie into the sewers where he gets what's coming to him. Out of nowhere, Katie reveals herself while tightly pulling Georgie in the neck. All the while, Dimov contacts Kirill to inform him of Katie's condition and her intention to seek vengeance on her attackers. He informs Kirill more about Katie, and the detective realizes that she is not completely safe. In the sewers, Georgie awakens to meet his fate, and sees Katie threatening him with the switchblade. She inflicts several wounds on Georgie while he is tied to ropes on the wall. While Georgie begs her to forgive him, Katie smears fecal matter on his wounds that would cause infection. He shouts endlessly, but it is music to Katie's ears. Finally, Katie has found a sense of satisfaction after days of struggle. Upon hearing Georgie in pain, she feels a slight sense of contentment that her plan worked out well. However, it is no match for what she has been through when they were in opposite positions. The scene is the beginning of how the film exposes Katie's unforgiving nature toward the men who wronged her. One night, Nikolai is out clubbing and an unknown person places a drink on his table. Nikolai grabs it, causing him to feel a sense of numbness and disorientation after realizing that the drink contains an illegal substance. He spots Katie from a distance, but he is too phased to think that she is real or not. Unhinged, he rushes to the bathroom and he sees an unlikely surprise. From across the bathroom, Katie stands as she reveals herself, causing Nikolai to throw up. While on his knees, Katie attacks Nikolai and smashes his head on the ground, she then drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the bathroom, Katie stands as she reveals herself, causing Nikolai to throw up. While on his knees, Katie attacks Nikolai and smashes his head on the ground. She then drags him to a dirty cubicle where she drowns him in an unflushed toilet. After carrying out another successful piece of revenge, Katie walks out of the club unseen. The following day, Valko attends church service, and he notices Katie from a corner. Before chasing her, he demands his family to go home at that moment. Velko runs to the basement, which gives Katie the perfect opportunity to strike him with a rock. He gains consciousness and finds himself strapped to a bed frame, surrounded by metal cables. She begins his ordeal by shocking him in several areas of his body, including his private parts. Katie then places a plumber's snake inside his mouth, causing the device to snake down to his throat as she continues to electrocute him. While Valko shouts in pain, Katie attaches electric cables to the bed frame and sets the regulator at a high voltage to kill him. Katie inflicts harm on Valko the same way he did to her. For her victims, the style and manner of her revenge have similarities to how she was mistreated before. As Valko used electroshock on her, she does the same thing to him, but with an added thrill. Just like the earlier film of the same name, the main character's revenge is always parallel to how they were taken advantage of. The franchise's formula for the films is the sweeter end of revenge. Later, Anna finds her house trashed with her stuff all over the place. She hears her music playing in the basement, which lures her there. Katie comes from behind to push Anna into the hole, which makes her unconscious. As Anna awakens, her face fills with horror at the sight of Georgie suffering from several infections. She screams upon seeing her bloody son helpless, but Katie finds no remorse in her actions. Instead, she covers the box that Anna is in after smashing the lid several times. Still clueless about the whole situation, Ivan drops by their house and is surprised to see it empty. The music also lures him to check on the basement where he is about to find something unexpected. Ivan is taken aback upon seeing the hole hollow and empty. In search of answers, he descends into the sewers to find his brother tied against the wall. As he attempts to wake Georgie up, Katie hits his head to knock him unconscious. Meanwhile, Father Dimov and Kirill talk about Katie and their growing concerns for her. The detective regrets not helping her as much as he could have when she was still in his custody. He makes use of the information that Katie is in the sewers and descends there as well. Although he did not participate in the crime, Kirill feels a sense of guilt. If he had only taken Katie seriously, since she approached him, she would be saved from more pain. If he gathered the ethic to drive Katie to the embassy, she would have been spared from more mistreatment. Father Dimov has put some sense into him that Katie's revenge is also a desperate call for help. Kirill admits to himself that he acted without urgency and he has to make up for it. After some time, Ivan wakes up to find himself tied to a table with Katie beside him. Ivan reveals that Anna is his stepmother, his father forced himself on her, and Georgie and Nikolai were products of the crime. Upon hearing this, Katie becomes more angered towards Anna for gearing her vengeful impulses on other women. To deepen Anna's pain, she removes Georgie's gag to show her her son's slow death before locking the box. Katie then focuses on inflicting harm on Ivan. She uses a mechanism that would crush his private parts, all the while making him realize her pain. In the process, Kirill arrives and holds Katie at gunpoint to stop her. Ivan suddenly grabs Katie from behind to strangle her, but Kirill lands a fatal shot on his head. It allows Katie to escape and gain her freedom while Kirill arrests Anna for her family's crimes. At last, Katie takes refuge in the American embassy, satisfied with her revenge. The film ends in favor of Katie, as she successfully overpowers the Patov family one by one. It also serves as a reminder of how the world is not a safe space, especially for women, and how they have to go the extra mile to protect themselves. Instead of peacefully asking for help, Katie morphs into a merciless Avenger, but after all, she is not the one to blame. Her way of getting back at the family is by delivering the same pain inflicted on her. After all, Katie's pain is incomparable to that of the Petov family.

The priest offers her food on his table, but Katie refuses to stay and spend time with him. Because of her decision, he lets her take food, clothes, and a Bible instead. At daybreak, Katie finally comes outside to make her way to the American Embassy. As she nears the building, she decides to turn back and stay. To Father Dimov's surprise, Katie is inside the church, so he approaches her. The priest recognizes her as a crime survivor, so he offers help and support. He suggests that they should contact authorities, but Katie stops him from doing so. When Katie exits, Father Dimov notices that she left a page of the Bible open with the phrase, Vengeance is mine. As a priest, Father Dimov highly values forgiveness. However, he does not force Katie to forgive anyone whenever they interact. He only reminds her of Christian values despite her obvious refusals. Father Dimov also does not question her about her struggles despite knowing what she has been through. The opening verse in the film also shows how Katie has faith but her hunger for revenge is greater. The following day Katie starts her plan for revenge. Her first step is to steal money from Anna's house to buy supplies and weapons. She does this successfully and moves on to her next ploy. Katie waits for Georgie to settle at a restaurant. She quickly walks past his table and puts down his drink. Moments later, Georgie sips his drink and notices that an object was inside the cup. To his surprise, Georgie sees Katie's crucifix pendant in his drink and realizes that she is alive. From a corner, Katie subtly reveals herself and Georgie follows her to make sure that she is actually real. Katie successfully lures Georgie into the sewers where he gets what's coming to him. Out of nowhere, Katie reveals herself while tightly pulling Georgie in the neck. All the while, Dimov contacts Kirill to inform him of Katie's condition and her intention to seek vengeance on her attackers. He informs Kirill more about Katie, and the detective realizes that she is not completely safe. In the sewers, Georgie awakens to meet his fate, and sees Katie threatening him with the switchblade. She inflicts several wounds on Georgie while he is tied to ropes on the wall. While Georgie begs her to forgive him, Katie smears fecal matter on his wounds that would cause infection. He shouts endlessly, but it is music to Katie's ears. Finally, Katie has found a sense of satisfaction after days of struggle. Upon hearing Georgie in pain, she feels a slight sense of contentment that her plan worked out well. However, it is no match for what she has been through when they were in opposite positions. The scene is the beginning of how the film exposes Katie's unforgiving nature toward the men who wronged her. One night, Nikolai is out clubbing and an unknown person places a drink on his table. Nikolai grabs it, causing him to feel a sense of numbness and disorientation after
realizing that the drink contains an illegal substance. He spots Katie from a distance,
but he is too phased to think that she is real or not. Unhinged, he rushes to the bathroom and

he sees an unlikely surprise. From across the bathroom, Katie stands as she reveals herself, causing Nikolai to throw up. While on his knees, Katie attacks Nikolai and smashes his head on the ground, she then drew a line to the ground, and then she drew a line to the ground, and

While on his knees, Katie attacks Nikolai and smashes his head on the ground. She then drags him to a dirty cubicle where she drowns him in an unflushed toilet. After carrying out another successful piece of revenge, Katie walks out of the club unseen. The following day, Valko attends church service, and he notices Katie from a corner. Before chasing her, he demands his family to go home at that moment. Velko runs to the basement, which gives Katie the perfect opportunity to strike him with a rock. He gains consciousness and finds himself strapped to a bed frame, surrounded by metal cables. She begins his ordeal by shocking him in several areas of his body, including his private parts. Katie then places a plumber's snake inside his mouth, causing the device to snake down to his throat as she continues to electrocute him. While Valko shouts in pain, Katie attaches electric cables to the bed frame and sets the regulator at a high voltage to kill him. Katie inflicts harm on Valko the same way he did to her. For her victims, the style and manner of her revenge have similarities to how she was mistreated before. As Valko used electroshock on

her, she does the same thing to him, but with an added thrill. Just like the earlier film of the same name, the main character's revenge is always parallel to how they were taken advantage of. The franchise's formula for the films is the sweeter end of revenge. Later, Anna finds her house trashed with her stuff all over the place. She hears her music playing in the basement, which lures her there. Katie comes from behind to push Anna into the hole, which makes her unconscious. As Anna awakens, her face fills with horror at the sight of Georgie suffering from several infections. She screams upon seeing her bloody son helpless, but Katie finds no remorse in her actions. Instead, she covers the box that Anna is in after smashing the lid several times. Still clueless about the whole situation, Ivan drops by their house and is surprised to see it empty. The music also lures him to check on the basement where he is about to find something unexpected. Ivan is taken aback upon seeing the hole hollow and empty. In search of answers, he descends into the sewers to find his brother tied against the wall. As he attempts to wake Georgie up, Katie hits his head to knock him unconscious. Meanwhile, Father Dimov and Kirill talk about Katie and their growing concerns for her. The detective regrets not helping her as much as he could have when she was still in his custody. He makes use of the information that Katie is in the sewers and descends there as well. Although he did not participate in the crime, Kirill feels a sense of guilt. If he had only taken Katie seriously, since she approached him, she would be saved from more pain. If he gathered the ethic to drive Katie to the embassy, she would have been spared from more mistreatment. Father Dimov has put some sense into him that Katie's revenge is also a desperate call for help. Kirill admits to himself that he acted without urgency and he has to make up for it. After some time, Ivan wakes up to find himself tied to a table with Katie beside him. Ivan reveals that Anna is his stepmother, his father forced himself on her, and Georgie and Nikolai were products of the crime. Upon hearing this, Katie becomes more angered towards Anna for gearing her vengeful impulses on other women. To deepen Anna's pain, she removes Georgie's gag
to show her her son's slow death before locking the box.
Katie then focuses on inflicting harm on Ivan.
She uses a mechanism that would crush his private parts,
all the while making him realize her pain.
In the process, Kirill arrives and holds Katie at gunpoint to stop her.
Ivan suddenly grabs Katie from behind to strangle her,

but Kirill lands a fatal shot on his head. It allows Katie to escape and gain her freedom while Kirill arrests Anna for her family's crimes. At last, Katie takes refuge in the American embassy, satisfied with her revenge. The film ends in favor of Katie, as she successfully overpowers the Patov family one by one. It also serves as a reminder of how the world is not a safe space, especially
for women, and how they have to go the extra mile to protect themselves. Instead of peacefully
asking for help, Katie morphs into a merciless Avenger, but after all, she is not the one to
blame. Her way of getting back at the family is by delivering the same pain inflicted on her.
After all, Katie's pain is incomparable to that of the Petov family.
